architecture.summary: MTL-LoRA augments standard LoRA by adding task-adaptive parameters that operate in the low-dimensional intrinsic space. During fine-tuning, the base pretrained model's weights are frozen; task-specific and shared LoRA modules project input features through low-rank bottlenecks (A, B matrices), and task-adaptive parameters learned in this projected space differentiate task-specific information while capturing shared knowledge across tasks. The mechanism allows a single pretrained backbone to jointly adapt to multiple target domains with limited trainable parameters.

biblio.title: MTL-LoRA: Low-Rank Adaptation for Multi-Task Learning
biblio.venue: Proceedings of the AAAI Conference on Artificial Intelligence

claims.statement: MTL-LoRA augments LoRA by incorporating additional task-adaptive parameters that differentiate task-specific information and capture shared knowledge across various tasks within low-dimensional spaces

LoRA tends to obscure the distinction between tasks by projecting sparse high-dimensional features from different tasks into the same dense low-dimensional intrinsic space, leading to task interference and suboptimal performance

claims.dataset: natural language understanding, commonsense reasoning, image-text understanding, and real-world industrial text Ads relevance datasets
claims.statement: MTL-LoRA outperforms LoRA and its various variants with comparable or even fewer learnable parameters in MTL setting
